events.description: Patents issued to Fred Rose, Ashley, Harris Tell, Edward Face Darkling, and Bull Tail
Next issuance of patents after 1915 to mixed blood and educated Indians
Initial patents issued to mixed bloods and educated Indians
Patent issued to full-blood Indian young man working for James R. Holsolaw (approximately two years prior to 1921)
Sale of land by patent-in-fee Indians mentioned in report dated July 9, 1921
Todd Smith sold approximately $13,000 worth of land over ten years
One man sold quarter section for $6,200 and bought another for $4,800
Another young man sold quarter for $4,000
Young woman sold quarter for $5,500 and bought another for $4,400
Woman sold 80 acres and bought another quarter
Indians mortgaging lands for automobiles and cash, losing land when loans default
Initial allotments made to heads of families under Enabling Act of 1905
Children allotted from fifty to seventy acres, often far from parents' homes

financial_transactions.amount: $750 cash plus two head of horses and two young heifers
Four head of horses and some money

financial_transactions.description: Sale of 740 acres total, money squandered, office purchased home with children's money
Sale of land, now living on children's money
Sale of quarter section, followed by purchase of another quarter for $4,800
Sale of quarter section, spent $1,800, investing remaining $3,300 in lands
Sale of quarter section, purchased another quarter for $4,400
Sale of 80 acres to purchase another quarter section
Sale of land over ten year period from original and inherited allotments
Sale of inherited patented land to purchase farming tools, tractor, plows and stock
Mortgages taken for automobiles, leading to foreclosure at less than actual value
Purchase of quarter section after selling other quarter for $6,200
Purchase of quarter section after selling original for $5,500
Purchase of home for Cantrells using their children's money

correspondence.subject: Conditions of patent-in-fee Indians on Fort Peck Reservation
correspondence.action_requested: Policy recommendations for future patent issuance, retention of homesteads
correspondence.outcome: Report submitted with examples of success and failure

correspondence.subject: Patent-in-fee Indians in Blackpipe District
correspondence.action_requested: Retain at least 100 acres for each Indian, strenuous examination for competency
correspondence.outcome: Report on 33 patents issued in district

correspondence.subject: Patent-in-fee Indians in Little White River District
correspondence.outcome: Report that all but four squandered land

correspondence.subject: Patent-in-fee Indians
correspondence.outcome: Recommendation against letting Indians sell all land, suggest 35-year retention

correspondence.subject: Patent-in-fee Indians on Rosebud Reservation
correspondence.outcome: Estimate 800 patents issued, only 10% success, recommend 40-acre retention

correspondence.subject: Patent-in-fee Indians
correspondence.outcome: List of 59 names submitted, observation that Indians must be placed on own resources

correspondence.subject: Patent-in-fee Indians (formerly Standing Rock Reservation)
correspondence.outcome: Report on Standing Rock patents, recommendation for individual examination

correspondence.subject: Patent-in-fee Indians on Rosebud and Standing Rock Reservations
correspondence.outcome: Comparison showing trust patent Indians better off than fee patent Indians

legislative_actions.outcome: Allotted lands to heads of families on Standing Rock Reservation
Allotted lands to wives of heads of families on Standing Rock Reservation
